key_insight: SevenNet-Polar adds accurate, fast prediction of Born effective charges to a SevenNet-based equivariant GNN, enabling charge-aware materials simulations alongside energy, forces, and stress.
engineering_relevance: The model makes electric-field-sensitive atomistic simulation much cheaper and more scalable, supporting practical studies of polarization, defects, interfaces, and dynamics in complex oxides.
method: Equivariant graph neural network built on the SevenNet architecture
Multitask training for energy, forces, stress, and Born effective charges
Uses FlashTP acceleration for large-scale GPU inference and simulation
evidence_signal: The abstract reports BEC-only RMSE as low as 0.0043 e and multitask performance of 1.0 meV/atom energy, 12 meV/angstrom forces, 0.05 GPa stress, and 0.0029 e BEC, with robust generalization to unseen NEB and grain-boundary environments.
watch_item: Performance may depend on coverage of local environments in the training set, and off-diagonal BEC components scale less favorably than energy, force, and stress errors.
paper_title: SevenNet-Polar for MultiTask Prediction of Energy, Forces, Stress, and Born Effective Charges: Development and Application to ZrO$_2$, Li$_3$PO$_4$, and Perovskites
